1100.s: After Kizaru arrives at the lab, St. Saturn contacts them and blackmails Vegapunk and Kuma with a list of demands to approve of Bonney being cured of Sapphire Scales including that Kuma become a cyborg with no memories, and a member of the Seven Warlords or Bonney will become a slave which Kuma gladly accepts. With a year and a half of treatment, Bonney spends six months with them, and then is sent back to Sorbet Kingdom for the remaining year with a member of CP8 keeping her under observation. Meanwhile, Kuma begins his life as a warlord and is sent an undisclosed mission to Foosha Village.

1101.s: Kuma watches a younger Luffy train and remembers a conversation he once had with Dragon and realizes that Luffy is Dragon's son. Kuma continues sending Bonney letters, unaware that Alpha had been intercepting and tearing them up. Conney has Bonney keep her powers a secret from Alpha. Just before Bonney's tenth birthday she is fully cured of her disease but Alpha refuses to let her to go outside. Having discovered that Alpha's group are government agents Conney enacts a plans for Bonney to escape on a ship. Alpha attempts to stop them, but Bonney remembering Kuma telling her about Nika, uses that power to stop Alpha.

1102.s:
As Bonney begins her career as a pirate with the intention of finding her father, Kuma hears of Luffy's exploits and realizes he is Dragon's son. As Kuma encounters the Straw Hat Pirates at both Thriller Bark and later Sabaody Archipelago, it is revealed the reason he separated the crew was because he knew they were not ready for the dangers of the New World, and he knows one day Luffy would save the world.

As Kuma begins the operation to have his memories removed, he creates a bubble containing his memories. However, as Kuma warned Vegapunk, when Bonney touched it, two years later, the bubble disappeared which left Bonney in tears when it happened.

1103.s: As Bonney is overcome with grief after experiencing her father's memories, Vegapunk gives her the birthday present Kuma had for her tenth birthday. During the present battle, Bonney attempts to attack Saturn with the power of Nika, but fails due to her doubts. When Bonney's attempt to affect Saturn fails, he reveals that both the fruits powers and her mother's illness were caused by him. Kuma then arrives and attacks Saturn and the Marines.

1104.s: As Kuma attacks Saturn, that latter is surprised that Kuma's self-destruct switch had not been activated yet which he first used after Kuma was sprung by the Revolutionary Army. Vegapunk reveals that he did not install one, but Kuma would have shutdown instead, but believes it may have something to do with his Buccaneer background. However, Saturn recovers and tries to eliminate Kuma and Bonney, but Sanji and Franky intervene. In response, Saturn orders a Buster Call on Egghead.

1105.s:
As the Marines prepare the Buster Call, Vegapunk attempts to give himself to no avail as Saturn does not believe the world needs progress. Sanji sends the rest of the group to Labophase while he will retrieve Vegapunk as he contacts Nami to warn the others about the Buster Call. Nami reports the situation from their end, including that Lilith and Brook have moved the Thousand Sunny. As the Marines begin their bombardment, Kizaru destroys the Vacuum Rocket that Franky's group is on.

As Saturn orders the Pacifistas to kill Kuma and Bonney, Luffy is discovered to be using the Food Machine to replenish himself. Meanwhile the Marine ship sent to kill the researchers that escaped Egghead is damaged and the surviving Marines onboard want to warn Kizaru that they failed in their mission and that an unknown party is heading to Egghead.

1106.s: As Luffy springs back into action with Gear 5, the Pacifistas attempt to kill Bonney and Kuma. Atlas then tells Bonney she orders the Pacifistas to help them escape, as Vegapunk secretly programmed their authority chip to follow her orders if she were in danger above that of the Five Elders. Kizaru tries to kill Kuma and Bonney, but is stopped by Luffy, to whom Vegapunk reveals to Bonney that he is Nika. Meanwhile, the new force is revealed to be the Giant Warrior Pirates, as Dorry and Brogy have come to save the Straw Hat Pirates.

1107.s: As the Giant Warrior Pirates begin their attack, Brook and Lilith move out with the Thousand Sunny while Jinbe heads to Zoro, who is still facing Rob Lucci. Luffy asks Bonney why she is crying, and she calls him Nika which he does not understand. Luffy then attacks Saturn, while Sanji fights Kizaru. Suddenly Devon and Augur show up, and are surprised to find Saturn, which brings them to state that their mission is now finished. On their way to leave the island, Caribou appears, and implores them to take him with.

1108.s: Augur does not trust Caribou, but the latter mentions he can tell Blackbeard about Poseidon and Pluton. As the Marines struggle against the Pacifistas, the Vice Admirals decide to target Bonney, and Dorry and Brogy save her from one of them. Meanwhile, Saturn assumes Beast Form, and when Sanji attempts to flee with Vegapunk, Kizaru kills the scientist. After his death, a pre-recorded message from Vegapunk then activates inside Egghead, as it broadcasts to tell the truth about the world.

1109.s: As the various places throughout the world prepare setting up their video Den Den Mushi, the other Elders contact Saturn telepathically to review the situation and they realize the signal is coming from Labophase which is protected by a barrier. Luffy continues his attack on Kizaru and Saturn, noticing the latter does not seem to be taking any damage from his attacks. Realizing they must stop the message, Saturn summons the other Elders to his location.

1110.s: While there is seven minutes left before the broadcast begins, the other members of the Five Elders have arrived at Egghead and have all utilized their Zoan abilities. Beginning their attack, Nusjuro disables the Pacifistas, allowing the Marines to focus on eliminating Bonney. Meanwhile after being contacted through a Den Den Mushi with an update, Jinbe, locates Zoro and Lucci, where after hearing Sanji's insult, Zoro quickly attacks Lucci. Meanwhile, Luffy is devoured by Ju Peter, but is saved by Dorry and Brogy.

1111.s: As Mars breaks into Labophase, Jinbe grabs Zoro and escapes back to the Thousand Sunny. Lucci then updates Mars on the situation. Luffy talks with Dorry and Brogy, and the two reveal he resembles the god in Elbaph's legends and their plan to escape where the other Straw Hat Pirates are. As the Giant Warrior Pirates enact their plan, Warcury attempts to stop Luffy from fleeing only to be stopped by Dorry and Brogy. After Luffy deals with Saturn's attack, all of a sudden the Iron Giant wakes up, as it apologizes to Joy Boy.

1112.s: As the remaining Pacifistas are disabled, the Marines move to deal with both Bonney and sink the Giant Warrior Pirates' ship. When York and Mars destroy the broadcast room but it does not stop the broadcast, York figures out that the Transmission Den Den Mushi that Dr. Vegapunk is using is in Punk Records, as Stussy reveals to Kaku that she intends to stay behind. At the Thousand Sunny Edison explains their plan as he goes to enact it. Meanwhile, Luffy reverts back to normal and has Dorry and Brogy provide him some food as they continue to fight Ju Peter and Warcury. While Saturn reaches the Thousand Sunny, Nusjuro reaches the Giant Warrior Pirates' ship and Mars reaches Punk Records.

1113.s: As the world prepares to listen to Dr. Vegapunk's upcoming speech, Sanji protects Bonney from Nusjuro. Oimo and Kashii join the fight. Mars learns the Den Den Mushi he found was a decoy. Saturn attacks Nami's group at the Thousand Sunny to murder Robin, but the other nearby Straw Hat Pirates members hold him back. As both Mars and York struggle to find the right Den Den Mushi without destroying the Power Plant, Vegapunk claims in his message that the world will sink into the Blue Sea.

1114.s: Various people around the world are shocked by Dr. Vegapunk's message, to which at Impel Down Doflamingo believes that most cities will sink once the sea rises five more meters. The Five Elders decide to destroy any life form to stop the transmission. As Saturn reaches the Power Plant where the Mother Flame is stored, Vegapunk confirms that this problem is not natural and is his first sin. He then reveals his second sin was researching the Void Century and tells the world about Joy Boy who was the very first pirate, as Edison finishes his plan for the Thousand Sunny to leave Egghead.

1115.s: As various places throughout the world react to Dr. Vegapunk's announcement of Joy Boy, Vegapunk reveals that back then the Twenty Kingdoms formed the World Government to counter Joy Boy leading to a war. Meanwhile Stussy refuses to leave Egghead due to Vegapunk's death and Zoro and Jinbe continue to head to the Thousand Sunny. Vegapunk reveals that the war ended with Joy Boy's defeat, but the war left permanent damage as the water level throughout the world rose 200 meters and the islands people live on are pieces of those continents. Vegapunk then reveals the cause of this are Ancient Weapons which still exist and waiting to be used which suggests the war never came to an end.

1116.s: As the world is shocked by Dr. Vegapunk's statement regarding the Ancient Weapons, he reveals the existence of the Mother Flame, the energy he created to power the world, and the fact that a portion of it was stolen. He then explains how the stolen portion was used to destroy the Lulusia Kingdom with an Ancient Weapon, and theorizes that when the day comes, the mystery of the Void Century will be revealed, revealing that only the Roger Pirates learned the truth. As York realizes he knew about her involvement in the Mother Flame theft, she realizes the Den Den Mushi that is transmitting the feed is on the Iron Giant and reports it to the Five Elders.

1117.s: As people throughout the world react to the information about how the sea level had risen 200 meters centuries ago at Marine GS General Hospital, the members of SWORD recover from their recent trip. At Egghead, Nusjuro attacks the Thousand Sunny, but Zoro with Jinbe's help intercepts him and tells Nami's group to sail off at once as they will jump on. Elsewhere, the Giant Warrior Pirates begin their own withdrawal as the Marines continue their assault. Just as Dr. Vegapunk is about to state the final part of his message regarding the Will of D., Warcury attacks the Iron Giant, causing the message to end.

1118.s: As the Iron Giant begins to sink beneath the ocean from the last attack, it wonders where Joy Boy is. The Five Elders decide their next target are both Lilith and Atlas. Luffy, Dorry, and Brogy arrive on the Giant Warrior Pirates' ship, and Sanji learns about the status of the Thousand Sunny. Mars attacks Luffy's group and after Luffy eats a meal from the giant's cook, he reactivates Gear 5. With Luffy's encouragement to help her, Bonney enters her Nika form as well. Hearing the Drums of Liberation causes the Iron Giant to reactivate.

1119.s: Kaku reluctantly allows for Stussy to escape. Luffy decides the only way to deal with the Five Elders is to knock them away from Egghead. Using teamwork his group knocks Mars away. Atlas decides to go check on the status of the Thousand Sunny. The video Den Den Mushi comes back online displaying more of the message, which York explains to the Elders they have to destroy it completely. Ju Peter releases the Cipher Pol agents from his body to try to suck up the Giant Warrior Pirates' ship forcing Luffy to stop him just as Bonney runs out of energy and reverts to her child form. Emet, the Iron Giant then stops Warcury's attack, as it states to Joy Boy to remember him when the time is right.

1120.s: In the past, it is revealed by Clover to Dr. Vegapunk that he is a D. and while Vegapunk initially refused to learn about the Void Century, he later decided to do so after he learned of Clover's death and Robin's survival in order to honor his friendship with Clover and Ohara's memory. In the present, as Vegapunk's message continues, Atlas knocks out Lilith and sacrifices herself so the Thousand Sunny can escape from Nusjuro. Meanwhile, the Iron Giant conveys a message to Luffy, as the other Elders decide to completely destroy it. Vegapunk then prepares to end his message as he states the twenty-five years ago, the Roger Pirates learned the true history.

1121.t: The Ebb and Flow of the Ages
1121.s: As the Giant Warrior Pirates defend against Saturn, Bonney regains her Nika form, she and Luffy knock away Saturn. As Dr. Vegapunk's message continues, he mentions on how history and fate are tied to elimination of the few living descendants of rare species. He believes that people should be to take care of themselves as they would head towards a new era. As the Thousand Sunny is landing towards the sea, Vegapunk believes the fate of the world is tied to whoever gets the One Piece.

1122.s: As more people react to Dr. Vegapunk's statement of the One Piece, Blackbeard agrees to meet Caribou to hear what he has to say, while the attack on the Iron Giant ends the transmission permanently. As the Iron Giant comments about Joy Boy still being alive, after Luffy corrects him and gives him his name, the Iron Giant asks him not to die as he releases a massive Haki attack which sends the Elders (minus Saturn) back to Mary Geoise, allowing the Thousand Sunny to land safely on the sea. A flashback occurs to when Joy Boy tells Emet to use that Haki he implanted him with when the time is right.

1123.s: With the Marines knocked out by the Haki burst, the Straw Hat Pirates and allies depart from Egghead and head to Elbaph. As Sanji looms over Vegapunk's body, a flashback occurs where after discovering a traitor among the satellites, Vegapunk, Shaka, and Pythagoras eventually learned that traitor was York, who had given both Vegapunk's knowledge of the Void Century, as well as the Mother Flame to the World Government. Realizing their doom, they secretly recorded their message and erased all knowledge of this discovery to help their plan succeed. It was before his death, Vegapunk expressed his wish to Sanji for his crew to find the One Piece.

1124.s: Vivi gets into a argument with Morgans when he decides to announce Luffy as Dr. Vegapunk's killer in the next newspaper considering there is no concrete proof. Meanwhile, Kizaru answers Sakazuki's call, as he asks the latter if he ever killed a close friend, remembers his past with Vegapunk. Meanwhile, on the Giant Warrior Pirates' ship, Lilith wakes up. She reveals to the Straw Hat Pirates that the other Vegapunks are technically still alive. The whole group then has a party, while on Elbaph a mysterious figure is waiting for the Straw Hat Pirates.

1125.s: As CP0 makes their return, while Saturn has a meeting with the Vice Admirals to evaluate the results of their mission. York discovers the Cloud Machines are active, resulting in Punk Records being moved into the sky. At Mary Geoise, while the World Nobles are suffering from the food shortage, Garling is given Saturn's position in the Five Elders, while Saturn himself is killed by Imu as punishment for allowing Luffy's escape. The consciousness and parts of the three destroyed Satellites are placed into a new body for Edison, while Dragon recognizes the repercussions of Vegapunk's message and knows they have to make a move before the world starts fighting over land.

1126.s: As both the Straw Hat Pirates and Giant Warrior Pirates celebrate, the Straw Hats learn from Oimo and Kashii how Dorry and Brogy came back to Elbaph from Little Garden. Meanwhile, the Red Hair Pirates have defeated the Barto Club and after Shanks puts Bartolomeo through a loyalty test, Yasopp destroys the Going Luffy-senpai. At Hachinosu, though Blackbeard is angry by Koby and Moria's escapes, he is satisfied with Garp's capture and the current state of the world as Catarina Devon's ship arrives with Caribou. Two days later, the Thousand Sunny disappears with six of the Straw Hats.

1127.t: Adventure in the Land of Mystery
1127.s: Strange occurrences are happening in the mysterious land, with a fire that threatens the tree Yggdrasil and the country as a whole. In Bigstein Castle, Nami is under attack by giant animals and runs into Usopp, saving him with Zeus. As they theorize on how they got there and consider hallucinations, they find the danger is real and are attacked by a monster that transforms into a giant lion, but are saved by Luffy, Zoro, and Sanji.

1128.s: As the Luffy's group continues on speculating where they are, they meet a man and learn they are in the Land of Gods which is ruled by the Sun God and warns them not to head in the other direction from the castle before leaving towards the Sun God's temple. As the group continues in that direction, they soon find they are actually in a giant-sized room and find Chopper. Upon reaching the temple they fight its guardians. Just as they capture one to use as transportation, the Sun God returns, furious by what he sees.

1129.s: As the Sun God is enraged by what he sees, the six Straw Hat Pirates move on, just before he catches up. With Nami's navigational skills she directs them where they need to go. The Sun God tries to catch them, but Zoro and Sanji breaks them out of the net. As the Sun God contemplates on what is happening, Luffy uses a modified version of Gear 4 to bust through the mirror in their way. It is revealed the Sun God is actually Road of the New Giant Warrior Pirates who was against their alliance with the Straw Hats. The Straw Hats then blast their way outside the diorama.

1130.s: At sea, the rest of the Straw Hat Pirates decide to call off the search for the Thousand Sunny, and head straight for Elbaph, knowing the other crew members are likely there. Meanwhile, the other crew members are exploring when they learn they are in Elbaph. While crossing a giant bridge, Luffy hears a voice and goes after it. As Franky's group learn that Hajrudin is a son of the king, and about the accursed prince Loki. On Elbaph, Luffy meets Loki.

1131.s: Luffy learns he is in the Underworld of Elbaph, where Loki has been imprisoned for over six years. Loki reveals that he had an encounter with Shanks, but refuses to reveal the details unless Luffy frees him. Meanwhile, the other Straw Hats on Elbaph are chased by Road with Gerd and Goldberg following. Hajrudin and Stansen are preparing a feast for the crew, while the Great Eirik nears Elbaph and Robin prepares for her reunion with Saul. Elsewhere, Saul has collapsed.

1132.s: As people throughout Elbaph are excited by the Great Eirik's arrival, the Giant Warrior Pirates take their ship up a Rainbow bridge. Luffy meets up with Gerd and Goldberg and together they rescue Nami's group from Road. As Dorry and Broggy explain to Franky's group about Elbaph's three levels, they arrive at a village as both groups reunite. A narration occurs from Louis Arnote not to stay in Elbaph for too long.

1133.t: I Want You to Praise Me
1133.s: A flashback occurs of Robin's life after the Ohara Incident. In the present, the Straw Hat Pirates, Bonney and Lilith learn of Elbaph's climate. As Luffy reunites with Hajrudin and his crew, they learn of Saul's condition. Upon arriving, Robin figures out he is faking it. Saul reveals that Vegapunk's words, Ohara's and his own sacrifice will lead to big changes in the future. The two then have a emotional reunion, with most of the Straw Hats crying in happiness.

1134.s: Luffy and Saul give their thanks to each other for what they did for Robin. Lilith then reveals that once she builds a new laboratory she can revive a clone of Vegapunk. Saul's assistant Ange then appears and offers to give a tour. During the tour the Straw Hats learn about the late King Harald. As Robin learns about the Owl Library, Loki talks to a mysterious individual and elsewhere two figures magically appear in the Elbaph village.

1135.s: As the Straw Hat Pirates check out the library, they encounter a giant owl to whom Lilith realizes has a Devil Fruit power. Luffy and Usopp are disappointed while playing with the children, as the giant children are no longer being trained to fight. Luffy is attacked by Colon, and they learn he is a half-giant. As the Straw Hats separate, the two strangers attack some guards and demand Loki's location. Luffy's group then have a toast of sake cups in honor for everything the Straw Hats did for the giants in the past. Jarul then appears and Luffy is surprised by the sword stuck in his helmet.

1136.t: The Land That Awaits the Sun
1136.s: As the Straw Hat Pirates converse with the Giants, they learn more about Nika and Loki, including that Shanks was the one who caught Loki. Luffy, Zoro and Nami have Road take them to Loki's location to determine if he knows where Shanks is. Meanwhile, the two strangers approach Loki, and are revealed they are there to recruit him to the Knights of God. When Loki refuses, the woman Gunko fights a giant wolf easily, as Loki states he will take all the negative emotions and use it to destroy the world.

1137.s:
Luffy's group enters Aurust Castle, where Road explains about the battle between King Harald and Loki.

In the Underworld, Gunko uses her Aro Aro no Mi abilities to inflict significant wounds on Loki, but the giant refuses to give in and join the Knights of God. Eventually, Loki and his animal companions are taken out and Gunko and her companion, Commander Figarland Shamrock, head out to make contact with Mary Geoise; Shamrock declares that Elbaph will be placed under the World Government's control.

1138.s:
When Loki demands to know Shamrock's connection to Shanks, the latter reveals Shanks is his twin brother. When Loki threatens him, Shamrock uses his sword to attack him, while Gunko provides their transportation to move out on their mission to capture one of the giants' children. Luffy finds it hard to believe that Shanks was the one who attacked the giant guards when he learns of it, to which the guards believe that Shanks might have a brother.

Meanwhile, as Franky learns from Ripley of ancient drawings from the Void Century, Saul provides Robin a book that details all of Elbaph's myths and legends, and details the past, present and future state of the world, as Robin reads each of the three chapters.

1139.s: While traveling to the throne room, Luffy's group encounters a Magic Circle. A voice is then heard by everyone, causing Road to believe it is a ghost, causing him to drop everyone. It turns out to be Gaban, a former member of the Roger Pirates, as well as Ripley's partner and Colon's father. Gaban then learns about the group's mission, including that Luffy plans to defeat Loki if he got out of control, stating only Shanks could previously do it. As other members of the Straw Hat Pirates learn of Gaban's strength, Luffy's group succeeds in finding the key to Loki's chains, to which Gaban decides to challenge Luffy to a fight for possession of the key.

1140.s: Gaban and Luffy engage in battle with each other, but Gaban decides to surrender and let Luffy take the key to Loki's chains. Meanwhile, Shamrock and Gunko arrive near the Walrus School and plan to capture the giant children, with the assistance of two more members of the Knights of God: Sommers and Killingham.

1141.s: Luffy's group leaves the Aurust Castle. He and Zoro go to Elbaph's Underworld to free Loki, while everyone else returns to the Western Village. Sanji and the New Giant Warrior Pirates arrive to try and stop Luffy and Zoro, but they are too late as Loki stands up from being partially released.

1142.s: As monsters from the Underworld are attacking the school, Saul and the principal fight them off, everyone watches as the children are being lured away with sleepwalking via one of Gunko's arrows as more monsters show up forcing the horn to warn the country to be blown. Meanwhile, Loki reveals he still has some power and demands the final chain be undone, claiming he wants to save Elbaph as he sets fire near Treasure Tree Adam. As Ange reveals an earlier incident, it is revealed that Killingham of the Knights of God is responsible for these events by exploiting the children's fears.

1143.s:
Jaguar D. Saul reaches the sleepwalking children, but cannot touch them as they are surrounded with invisible thorns generated by Sommers' Iba Iba no Mi ability. A firefighting svarr crew moves to put out the fire Loki started, but are swiftly knocked out of the air by one of the nightmares manifested by Killingham's Ryu Ryu no Mi, Model: Kirin mythical power, and the children walk straight toward the blaze.

Down in the Underworld, Luffy moves to fight Loki in response to his threats, only for Loki to collapse from his wounds. Gerd gives him medical treatment, though Hajrudin is inclined to kill his brother to avenge their father; however, Loki throws doubt on the narrative that he committed premeditated murder on King Harald.

1144.s: Franky and Ripley reach a scientific facility and reunite with Lilith and Bonney; Lilith estimates that the facility is 3000 years old. However, they then come under attack by the MMA along with the Western Village. Nami informs Jarul of the report of two hostile intruders at Aurust Castle, and she, Usopp, Brook, and Jinbe go to search for them on a svarr commandeered by Colon. As Saul tries to figure out a way to stop the sleepwalking children, he comes under attack by a fenrir MMA, but soon afterward the Giant Warrior Pirates and Scopper Gaban arrive to battle all the MMA.

1145.t: Wildfire in Forest Sector 2, Branch Route 8
1145.s: As Luffy's group prepares food for Loki in order for him to regain his strength, they learn from him about the Knights of God. When Loki learns that Luffy idolizes Shanks, he reveals to him that he had similarly idolized Rocks D. Xebec long ago. Meanwhile as the Giant Warrior Pirates and Gaban fight off the MMAs, Jinbe deals with the fire around Treasure Tree Adam, saving the kids from walking into it. However, Gunko appears before them, targeting Colon.

1146.s: Gunko defeats the Straw Hat Pirates on their Svarr and takes Colon as a hostage. Killingham invades the Walrus School and takes control of its transmission system. As Sommers takes down Saul, he criticizes King Harald's decision to change Elbaph from its populace of warriors to pacifists.

1147.s: The Knights of God broadcast their identity across Elbaph, revealing they are responsible for these events, and have the children wake from their dream. Sommers addresses Jarul that their goal is to make the giants their slaves in battle. As the children's identities are revealed, Sommers demands that the school and library books be destroyed, as both Robin and Gaban prepare to stop them.

1148.s: The Knights of God select one of the giant children Ronja to be killed unless Jarul gives in to their demands. While Franky, Ripley and the Giant Warrior Pirates split into two groups to deal with the MMA's and saving the children, Robin confronts Sommers alone. Using one of her clones, Robin manages to force Sommers to fall into the Underworld. As Jarul reluctantly orders Ange to burn down the school and library, Sommers returns and overpowers Robin, before Gaban arrives and attacks Sommers, causing him to lose an arm.

1149.s: Gaban swiftly overpowers Sommers and decapitates the Knight at the shoulders which he does not regenerate from. Ange sets fire to the Walrus School while Killingham sets the Owl Library ablaze. As Gaban prepares to extinguish the blaze, Colon calls out to him, causing Gunko to go after the giant boy. Foreseeing that the attack on his son will be fatal, Gaban surrenders and Gunko strikes him with several arrows, sending him falling into the Underworld. However, Gunko is then overcome by a memory of her past concerning Brook, but as she heads toward him she is suddenly possessed by Imu.

1150.s: Imu, possessing Gunko's body, unleashes a massive burst of Supreme King Haki and takes out several giants and giant children. Killingham tells Imu he has still left some nightmare holes open and thus more MMA's will keep coming in. Sommers heals from his injuries. Dorry and Brogy arrive at the scene, with Brogy grabbing Imu but Imu shoots Brogy's arm off using a huge pistol and uses a technique called Domi Reversi to forcibly make a contract with Brogy that transforms him into a new gigantic demonic form, and does similar contracts with Brogy and several members of the Giant Warrior Pirates. Imu commands Dorry and Brogy to kill Jarul and proclaim themselves as the new Kings of Elbaph, while Jarul refuses to heed warnings to run.

1151.s: As the chaos continues with the rampage caused by the demons and other monsters, Imu reveals to Killingham their plans to continue until the "Heart of Elbaph" has been subjugated. A flashback of the events from a few minutes ago occurs, where Chopper was able to save Gaban when he was falling and both had ended up in the Underworld where Luffy's group is. Upon learning of both Gaban's true identity, and the situation in Sun World, Hajrudin orders this crew to prepare to return to Sun World so they could save Elbaph, to which Loki states they will need his help for this situation.

1152.s: As Luffy's group prepares to depart for Sun World, Gaban mentions on how Supreme King Haki can be effective against the Knights of God. With Hajrudin refusing to release Loki, some of the group go up to Sun World first. Once Hajrudin permits Loki to tell him the events of the incident 14 years ago, it occurs when Loki and Jarul come across a demon whom Jarul realizes is King Harald, as a mysterious figure locates a forbidden Devil Fruit. Another flashback occurs to 109 years ago, where back then Harald was more warlike, having just ascended the throne at the giant age of 45.

1153.s: King Harald began turning away from his arrogance and bloodlust after meeting the South Blue giant Ida, and 105 years ago he began working to improve diplomacy between Elbaph and the rest of the world. He fathered Hajrudin with Ida 81 years ago, but this was not recognized due to her outsider status. Harald was then betrothed to Estrid, who gave birth to their son Loki 18 years later. Estrid was terrified of Loki's demonic-looking appearance and threw him into the Underworld, intending to declare he had been stillborn. However, the newborn Loki climbed back up the Treasure Tree Adam. Estrid then fell ill, and all misfortunes that fell on Elbaph since then were attributed to a curse on Loki.

1154.s:
In the winter following Loki's birth, Jorul and Estrid died as Elbaph suffered under severe weather events and a famine, causing many conflicts to break out. However, Harald returned to the island and restored stability as his diplomatic efforts resulted in many countries delivering aid to the giant kingdom. Seven years later, Harald spoke with Jarul about his difficulty dealing with the World Government; Jarul told him about a shipwright group called Galleila that was somehow captured long ago, suggesting the giants possess an ancient enemy.

Eight years following this, Elbaph was prosperous under Harald's diplomatic rule. Loki, still revering the old ways, regularly rampaged around the kingdom and bullied Hajrudin, though was ultimately doing this to try to fill a void of love that Estrid had not given him and he even attempted to kill himself at points. One day however, Loki encountered a band of pirates led by Rocks D. Xebec, the father of Blackbeard. Rocks was looking for Harald, who eight years earlier had witnessed him murder a Marine Admiral at the Levely.

1155.s: 56 years ago, after clashing with King Harald at the Levely, Rocks became notorious across the world for his attacks against the World Government, and began recruiting powerful pirates. Eight years later at Elbaph, Rocks attacked Loki to force a meeting with Harald, and then spoke of his plans against the government. After Harald refused to join him, Rocks officially formed his crew and began his ambition to take over the world.

1156.s: Four decades ago, the Kuja Pirates led by Gloriosa and Shakuyaku were one of the most prominent pirate crews in the world, and they came into conflict with the youthful Roger Pirates as well as the Marines under Garp's command. On Elbaph, a resentful Loki repeatedly tried to escape and asked Rocks to take him as a crewmate during the pirate's repeated visits to recruit Harald, but was always rebuffed. The Rocks Pirates had successfully taken over Pirate Island and continued attracting powerful members. Rocks was intent on getting Harald to accept his invitation in order to eat a particular Devil Fruit, and the crew was also interested in finding the Galleila giants as they plotted to utilize the might of Elbaph to overthrow Mary Geoise.

1157.s: As the Rocks Pirates continue wreaking havoc on the seas, their notoriety only gets higher despite having been together for only two years. Rocks continues trying to recruit Harald to join his crew but Harald keeps turning him down in order to make Elbaph a member of the World Government, despite running into complications. Shakuyaku quits as the captain of the Kuja Pirates, which shocks the entire world. Shakuyaku then arrives on Hachinosu and sets up a bar on the island, with many of the pirates, even members of the Rocks Pirates, being enamored by her, and they allow her to stay. The Five Elders declare they are willing to allow Elbaph to be a member nation on one condition: the warriors of Elbaph must kill Rocks.

1158.s: After Rayleigh received a letter from Shakuyaku, the Roger Pirates decide to visit her at her bar on Hachinosu. While there, they met Rocks and his crew, though the rules of the bar prevented any fighting. At Elbaph, Harald and Rocks engage in a battle, but after it ends in a draw, their friendship comes apart and Rocks gives up on trying to recruit Harald. Since then, they would never meet again until the day of a certain incident. A year later at God Valley, the twin brothers Shanks and Shamrock are born.

1159.s: Pirates everywhere react in distress after learning Shakuyaku had disappeared from Hachinosu. A year later, Garling leads the Marines, including Dragon, to God Valley to round up the country's citizens and prepare for the Native Hunting Competition. The news leaks that Shakuyaku is held at God Valley during the event, prompting the Rocks Pirates and Roger Pirates to go rescue her, with Garp on his way as well. The Rocks Pirates are confronted by Harald during their trip, but Rocks decides to talk with him, where he reveals to Harald his connection to the legendary pirate Davy D. Jones and the Davy Family. Rocks states his true motive is to save his wife and child on God Valley, but after Harald refuses to help him, he attacks Harald's ship. At God Valley, the World Nobles gather for the competition, with Shakuyaku as the grand prize, and the targets including Teach, the first member of the Davy Family identified in 800 years.

1160.s: After members of the Davy Family are revealed, the World Nobles begin the Native Hunting Competition to hunt down their targets throughout God Valley. The competition is disrupted by the arrival of the Rocks Pirates, Roger Pirates, and several other pirate ships. During the chaos, Dragon attempts to get Shanks and Shamrock off of God Valley. The Knights of God begin to take action and prepare for battle. While the Marines are being attacked, Garp and his fleet arrive to take on the pirates.

1161.t: A Song of Love Bound Under a Hail of Arrows
1161.s: While Dragon is on the move, Shamrock is taken from him by Satchels Maffey, leaving him to rescue only Shanks. The Rocks Pirates are ambushed by other pirate crews and Garp as they make their way to the center of God Valley at the same time as the Roger Pirates. During the fight, Shepherd Sommers attempts to kill Shakuyaku but is stopped when Silvers Rayleigh attacks him and frees Shakuyaku, and the Roger Pirates begin their escape.

1162.s: While Silvers Rayleigh flees with Shakuyaku, all of the pirates continue fighting each other for the treasure at the center of God Valley. Rocks D. Xebec helps Eris and Teach escape the island while he stays to fight back against the Knights of God and his family members that were turned into demons. Once Charlotte Linlin gains possession of the Uo Uo no Mi, Model: Seiryu, Kaidou attacks her and steals the fruit for himself to eat. Shortly after Bartholomew Kuma helps the citizens and slaves leave God Valley, Imu emerges from Jaygarcia Saturn and confronts Rocks, Edward Newgate, and Kaidou.

1163.s: Imu possessing Saturn attacks both Marines and pirates nearby. Rocks confronts Imu and attacks them while speaking of a certain "promise". Captain John is revealed to have the Jiki Jiki no Mi and tries to leave with all the treasure but Ganzui attacks him with the Bomu Bomu no Mi and also attacks Shiki who tried to get the treasure. While Linlin attempts to attack Kaidou, Garp, Roger, Rocks, Newgate, Kaidou and Linlin all attack Imu simultaneously with powerful Supreme King Haki infused attacks, only for Imu to simply regenerate back and recover. Dragon loses Shanks and Shanks climbs into an open chest filled with treasure. Imu tells Rocks that they will make him kill his own family and tranforms him into a demon using Domi Reversi.

1164.s: Rocks resists his transformation and taunts Nerona Imu about Davy D. Jones and Joy Boy, which enrages Imu. Imu declares that he will subjugate Elbaph and Harald, much to Rocks' anger, and once again tells him to kill his family as Rocks' transformation completes. Rocks, in his demonic form, attacks Kaidou and Big Mom, and clashes with Whitebeard, destroying the mountain on God Valley. Rocks' nearly kills his own wife and son, but Kuma manages to teleport them and himself away from God Valley. Rocks kills several people till Garp and Roger confront him to stop him. They hear Rocks' voice, begging them to kill him.

1165.s: The remaining Rocks Pirates and Roger Pirates make their escape from God Valley. Garp and Roger team up to fight Rocks, initially struggling to bypass his defenses. When the Marines evacuate, Imu orders for all records of God Valley to be kept secret. Garp and Roger decide that only a critical mass of Supreme King Haki will be enough to deal damage, so they launch their combined attacks against Rocks, defeating him.

1166.s: As God Valley begins sinking into the ocean, Garling kills Rocks while Gaban and Rayleigh escape with Roger and Garp. Later, the Roger Pirates discover an infant Shanks inside one of the treasure chests looted from the island. News of the Rocks Pirates' defeat spreads quickly, framing Garp as a hero, much to his frustration. Loki is completely devasted by news of Rocks' death. Garp visits Dragon in his jail cell, where the latter declares that he is quitting the Marines and tells his father he despises him. Harald, overwhelmed with shame and grief for abandoning Rocks to die for the sake of his mission of peace for Elbaph, travels to Marineford and tears off his horns as a symbol of his commitment to a peaceful future.

1167.s: Five years after the God Valley Incident, Harald had assisted the World Government in eliminating pirates throughout the Grand Line, eventually becoming a Devoted Blade in the Knights of God. In the same year that Fisher Tiger had helped the World Nobles' slaves escape Mary Geoise, Harald meets with Shanks, who had returned to Mary Geoise and also became a Devoted Blade. In Elbaph, Ida became ill after being poisoned by the elders from Estrid's hometown, prompting Loki to attack them and set the village ablaze.

1168.s: Elbaph's reputation improves around the world as Giants join the Marines. Ida passes away on a snowy day. Loki was locked up for burning Brewers village and mourns Ida silently. Since Shanks went missing while completing a mission right before being promoted to a Knight of God, Harald is chosen instead. Harald meets Imu who grants him immortality and makes him a Knight of God. Imu orders Harald to form an army of Giant warriors for the Navy, which Harald refuses. Imu says as part of the Depths Covenant, Harald will receive immortality and power but must obey every order. Harald, realizing he is losing control, asks the guards to chain him to a pillar and call Loki and Jarul to Aurust Castle.

1169.t: My Death Cannot Come Soon Enough
1169.s: When Harald starts losing control over his body to Imu, he tells the guards to kill him, but his immortality prevents any damage. After Loki and Jarul show up, Harald tells Loki to take Elbaph's legendary Devil Fruit, kill him, and take over the throne. He soon loses control and attacks all of the guards, and stabs Jarul through his helmet, but Shanks and Gaban arrive and stop him from going after Loki. Loki finds the Devil Fruit but is suddenly attacked by Ragnir, which moves on its own.

1170.s: Harald reveals nobody could eat Elbaph's legendary Devil Fruit because Ragnir did not choose anyone. Gaban and Shanks continue trying to stop Harald. Gaban reveals the Knights of God tried to take Shanks back several times. Harald continues causing destruction. Ragnir is revealed to have "consumed" a Squirrel-type Zoan Devil Fruit. Ragnir allows Loki to eat the Devil Fruit. Loki transforms and severely injures Harald, temporarily bringing him back to his senses. Loki tearfully unleashes a powerful lightning attack on Harald, shattering him to pieces.

1171.s: After reluctantly killing Harald, Loki takes the surviving guards with him out to sea, asking Jarul, Gaban, and Shanks to keep the truth a secret. Returning to the present, Hajrudin forgives Loki for the circumstances of Harald's death and releases him. As everyone else moves up from the Underworld, Loki demonstrates to Luffy his power by attacking a Draugr MMA.

1172.s: Gaban informs Zoro, Sanji, Chopper and the New Giant Warrior Pirates about the Knights of God that are attempting to abduct Elbaph's children. When they get to the Sun World, they split into groups to save the children, stop the demonized Giant Warrior Pirates from killing Jarul, and save the captured Straw Hat Pirates near the Walrus School and Owl Library. Jarul makes an announcement to Elbaph, acquitting Loki of murdering Harald and encouraging the giants to protect Elbaph from being controlled by the World Government.

1173.s: Zoro attacks several of the demon Giant Warrior Pirates from above and gets Dorry and Brogy to accidentally strike each other. Meanwhile, Brook calls out to Gunko, whom he recognizes as the spitting image of a princess he once knew named Shuri. This triggers Gunko's repressed memories and causes her arrows to loosen, forcing Imu to fight to maintain possession of her. Nearby, Sommers has almost brought the Walrus School children to an escape ship when the children's parents arrive to rescue them. A Nika MMA comes and attacks, but is brought down by Gerd, Goldberg, and Road, destroying the ship in the process.

1174.t: The Strongest Thing in the World
1174.s: Sommers watches as the Walrus School children are forced to walk off the branch to certain death. With no way to stop the arrow taking them off the edge, the children's family members embrace them so they will fall off together. However, they make a fortuitous landing on Luffy atop a transformed Loki's back. When Loki flies up to the Sun World, both snow and lightning are summoned, and Luffy enters Gear 5 as the duo prepare to join the fighting.

1175.s: As Sanji frees Robin and Loki deposits the giant children and parents at Western Village, upon learning from Loki's announcement of the children surviving, Sommers' plan to recapture them turns into an assassination attempt when they chose to protect their parents. Zoro blocks Sommers' attack and Luffy using a Gear 5 Haki technique takes down the Knight of God, with Loki then crushing him. While observing the battle, Gaban recalls the day he learned about Loki's Devil Fruit ability with Shanks from Jarul, as Imu is surprised that the Devil Fruit had been in Elbaph all along.

1176.s: The Straw Hat Pirates arrive to extinguish the fires at the Owl Library and Walrus School, with Lilith using "ODCs" to freeze them both solid. They proceed to fight against Killingham to prevent him from destroying the library and school. At Elbaph's Western Village, Dorry and Brogy slowly regain control over their demon bodies and decapitate each other, but this process instead turns them back to normal. Realizing this, they join Zoro, Hajrudin, and Stansen in fighting the demonized giants and removing their demon transformations as well.

1177.s: Zoro and the giants at the Western Village decimate the demonized giants with ease, while Chopper protects Kashii by punching one of the demonized giant using his Monster Point form, which inadvertently drives the demon away and restores the giant back to normal. Killingham is captured by the Straw Hats at the Owl Village as Luffy and Loki briefly reunite with them. Meanwhile, Usopp attempts to rescue Brook from Imu but is easily defeated. Though the resulting explosion draws the attention of Luffy and Loki who confront Imu, who remarks that the ensuing battle will inevitably divide the world.

1178.s: Luffy and Loki fight against the Imu-possessed Gunko, resulting in Imu being frozen by Loki. Nami, Jinbe, and Lilith continue to extinguish fires across Elbaph while Zoro and Chopper restore all the demonized giants back to normal. Robin and Saul find out that the books at the Owl Library and Biblo have disappeared. Imu fades away from the ice block and returns to Mary Geoise, where they inform the Five Elders that they will take a leave for a moment.

1179.s: The Five Elders have great unease about Imu going down to the surface world, but the ruler sees no other choice given the machinations of history. As the Straw Hats collect themselves following their subjugation of the Knights of God, they witness Imu arriving at Aurust Castle, with the Abyss surrounding it with large black flames and bringing the trees and buildings to life. Using the power Akuma no Mi, Imu heads to the castle roof as he is revealed to be Nerona Imu, one of the First Twenty and the King of the World.

1180.s: In his search for the King of Elbaph, Imu descends to the Western Village, freeing Sommers and overpowering Zoro and the other giants. Luffy and Loki make it to the Walrus School to consume more food before heading out to fight. Imu then frees Killingham while taking down Sanji, before he is confronted by Loki, who admits to Imu that he killed Harald.

1181.s: Imu fends off and injures Loki with his "Omen" ability, but Loki is able to retaliate by striking him into the Treasure Tree Adam with Ragnir. Loki then transforms into the Nidhöggr, and Imu cracks a smile upon seeing it again.

1182.s: Loki and Ragnir continue their battle against Imu. Sommers and Killingham return to their mission to abduct Elbaph's children as Zoro and Sanji try to stop them. Inside the Western Village, Killingham summons a MMA resembling the mythical God of Rain Zaza to kidnap the children and flood the village.

1183.s: Imu and Loki continue their battle as Killingham takes seven children from the giants. Meanwhile, the Straw Hats ask Brook about his connection to Gunko and what to do with her, which leads him to recall his past as an elite soldier of the Esperia Kingdom.

1184.t: We're All Bones When We Die
1184.s: As Shuri reached 14 years old, her beauty caught the attention of princes of the neighboring kingdoms. After rejecting their advances, Shuri and Brook sing "We're All Bones When We Die", a song from his childhood that reminded him of when then-Prince Reuven saved him from his troubled years.

1185.s: After recounting his past under Candelle's tutelage, Brook and Shuri continued to enjoy their day. However, one year later, the Esperia Kingdom was plagued with a great smog that killed Queen Candelle and drove the country into poverty. Unable to raise the Heavenly Tribute for that year, King Reuven refused to send 1,000 slaves to the World Nobles as a compromise and declared war against the World Government. After Brook fought his way past the Marines to rescue Shuri, he instead encountered the now demonized Shuri, who apparently murdered her own father in presence of two mysterious figures.

1186.s: After revealing that Shuri was in fact descended from the World Noble bloodline and what transpired on the day that King Reuven died, Brook asks his Straw Hats crewmates to free her from Ragnir's ice before heading off somewhere. Meanwhile, Luffy has finally regained his strength and joined Loki in the fight against Imu.

1187.s: Luffy resumes his battle against Imu as Loki is taken down by the latter. Panicked by its master's injuries, Ragnir unknowingly melts the ice block with Gunko inside. Meanwhile, Zoro and Sanji confront Sommers and Killingham, respectively, with Sanji recalling Gaban's advice on how to awaken his Supreme King Haki in order to counter Killingham's immortality.

1188.s: As Imu reminisences about Joy Boy due to Luffy's resemblance to him, he ramps up the pressure to force Luffy on the defensive. In turn, Luffy unveils the Gomu Gomu no Majin technique in an attempt to counter Imu. After Imu is briefly distracted by Brook, he manifests a sword named Bohu and stabs Luffy with it, as he calls Luffy not the same as Joy Boy due to him being far stronger than Luffy is right now.
